title: Infinite URL Spaces, Calendars, Facets, Sessions, and Budget Defense · Type Forensics
summary: A preserved TypeScript crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, Fetch, stream, Cheerio, selector, frontier, Promise, browser, axe, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in URL-space trap classifier and containment policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Detect calendar, pagination, faceting, sorting, search, session, nonce, path-depth, repeated-segment, and parameter-combination expansion.
Budget distinct keys, values, combinations, orderings, and per-template pages with stakeholder-approved exceptions.
Classify soft 404, login, challenge, empty shell, redirect loop, and repeated-template responses using multiple signals.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Trap Patterns
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript team handles case 8765 during a multilingual archive markup migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to contain calendar, facet, session, template, soft-error, and query-cardinality traps with stop evidence; release evidence binds source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, lockfile, package, browser revision, container, configuration, and restored state; then reorder one frontier, p-limit, Promise, AbortSignal, stream, retry, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Detect calendar, pagination, faceting, sorting, search, session, nonce, path-depth, repeated-segment, and parameter-combination expansion.

Inspect the Trap Patterns trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Trap Patterns.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for detect calendar, pagination, faceting, sorting, search, session, nonce, path-depth, repeated-segment, and parameter-combination expansion. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Trap Patterns, keep Trap Patterns connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

stimulus.title: Trap Patterns case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A debug TypeScript team handles case 8765 during a multilingual archive markup migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to contain calendar, facet, session, template, soft-error, and query-cardinality traps with stop evidence; release evidence binds source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, lockfile, package, browser revision, container, configuration, and restored state; then reorder one frontier, p-limit, Promise, AbortSignal, stream, retry, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Competency probe: Detect calendar, pagination, faceting, sorting, search, session, nonce, path-depth, repeated-segment, and parameter-combination expansion.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Duplicate URL strings are the only cause of infinite crawls.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Detect calendar, pagination, faceting, sorting, search, session, nonce, path-depth, repeated-segment, and parameter-combination expansion. Artifact under review: URL-space trap classifier and containment policy.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the inspect the failure record evidence boundary.

For inspect the failure record, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Trap Patterns, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
